worth_it_explanation: Amazon DSP sends traffic directly to PDPs. A campaign that drives clicks to a PDP with incomplete content, an out-of-stock listing, or a thin review profile hands the spend to a page that cannot convert. These checks take a few minutes to run in the Amazon DSP console and in the product listing itself. The fixes, whether adding images, correcting the content gap, or addressing a Buy Box eligibility issue, sit upstream of the campaign but resolve a conversion failure that no bid adjustment can overcome.
intro: Amazon DSP routes ad traffic to product detail pages (PDPs) on Amazon.com. For endemic advertisers, the PDP is where the sale happens or fails. A campaign can generate qualified clicks and still produce no purchases if the landing page is incomplete, the product is out of stock, a third-party seller has claimed the Buy Box, or the review count and star rating are low enough to deflect the buyer. These checks confirm that the PDPs being sent traffic are in a state that supports conversion.

audit_checks.title: Product detail pages sent traffic by the campaign have incomplete content
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Clicks from Amazon DSP land on the product detail page, not a brand-controlled landing page. A PDP missing a descriptive title, product images below Amazon's recommended count, or no A-plus content gives the buyer less to work with than a competing PDP with full content. Ad spend drives traffic to a page that cannot close the sale.

educate.base:
Amazon DSP directs ad traffic to a product detail page (PDP) on Amazon.com. The Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN) tied to the ad creative is the landing page. Unlike a brand-controlled site where the advertiser can optimize the landing page independently, the PDP's quality depends on the content the seller or vendor has loaded in Seller Central or Vendor Central.

Amazon's own guidance holds that a PDP needs a clear, descriptive title, a minimum of six high-resolution product images, bullet points covering key features, and a product description or A-plus content module. A-plus content, which allows brands to add enhanced images, comparison charts, and formatted copy, is associated with higher conversion rates on PDPs that qualify for it.

When a PDP is missing these elements, a buyer who clicks from a DSP ad arrives at a page with less information than competing listings. The click was paid for, but the page cannot support the purchase decision the campaign was designed to drive.

A few signs point to incomplete PDP content:
  • The ASIN's title is generic or truncated rather than descriptive
  • The PDP shows fewer than six product images, or images are low-resolution
  • No A-plus content module is present on ASINs that are eligible for it
  • Bullet points are absent or describe only one or two product attributes
You may want to review each ASIN the campaign sends traffic to before evaluating click-to-detail-page-view rate or conversion rate. A low rate on either can trace to the PDP rather than the ad.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For endemic advertisers, the ASIN-level PDP is the destination for every DSP click that goes to Amazon.com. You may want to confirm that each ASIN being promoted has a title, image set, and description that reflect the product accurately. Amazon's Responsive eCommerce Creative (REC) format pulls the ASIN's product image and title directly from the listing, so a weak PDP also produces a weaker ad unit.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Non-endemic advertisers drive clicks to off-Amazon destinations rather than product detail pages on Amazon.com. This check does not apply to non-endemic campaigns.

learn_more.source_title: eCommerce display creatives: guidelines and ad specs

investigate.intro: Review the PDP for each ASIN the campaign is actively sending traffic to. Confirm title quality, image count, and whether A-plus content is present.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Open the Amazon DSP console and navigate to the line item or creative you want to review. Note the ASIN or ASINs the creative is pointing to.

Pull the detail-page-view rate (DPVR) and, if available, the add-to-cart or purchase rate for the line item. A DPVR that is lower than expected relative to click-through rate can indicate a gap between the click and the page load, but a normal DPVR alongside a low purchase rate points toward PDP content or pricing.

amazon_product_listing.label: Amazon product listing (manual review)
steps: Open the product listing on Amazon.com for each ASIN the campaign promotes.
Check the title: confirm it is descriptive and includes the product type, brand, and key attributes rather than a placeholder or a short generic label.
Count the product images. Note whether there are at least six images, whether they are high-resolution, and whether lifestyle or detail shots are present alongside the main product image.
Scroll to the product description. Confirm bullet points cover more than one or two attributes and that a product description or A-plus content module is present below the fold.
Note any ASINs where these elements are absent or incomplete.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per ASIN the campaign sends traffic to, whether the title is descriptive, whether the image count meets or exceeds six, whether bullet points are present, and whether a product description or A-plus content module is present. An ASIN that meets all four criteria passes. An ASIN missing any of them is a fail recorded with the ASIN and the specific gap observed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Review each ASIN above and record the title quality, image count, and A-plus content status to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more ASINs receiving traffic from {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP campaign have incomplete product detail page content. A PDP missing a descriptive title, sufficient images, or A-plus content gives buyers arriving from the campaign less to evaluate than a competing listing with full content, which reduces the probability that the click converts to a purchase. {{voice_recommend}} auditing each promoted ASIN for title quality, image count, bullet point coverage, and the presence of A-plus content before the next campaign flight. Addressing content gaps on the PDP is upstream of bid or budget changes and resolves a conversion barrier that the campaign itself cannot compensate for.

audit_checks.title: Advertiser does not hold the Buy Box on promoted ASINs
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When a third-party seller or Amazon itself holds the Buy Box on a promoted ASIN, the 'Buy Now' and 'Add to Cart' buttons route the purchase to that seller rather than the advertiser. The DSP campaign pays for the click, the buyer arrives at the PDP, and the sale goes to a competitor or Amazon. The advertiser bears the full ad cost with no revenue benefit.

educate.base:
Amazon's Buy Box (called the 'Featured Offer' in Seller Central) is the 'Buy Now' and 'Add to Cart' section that appears on the right side of a product detail page. When a buyer clicks 'Buy Now', the purchase goes to whichever seller currently holds the Buy Box. Amazon assigns the Buy Box based on factors including price competitiveness, fulfillment method, seller rating, and inventory availability. A listing can have multiple sellers competing for it, and the Buy Box rotates or goes to the lowest-eligible offer.

For an Amazon DSP campaign promoting an ASIN, the advertiser needs to hold the Buy Box for the campaign to drive purchases to their own account. If a third-party seller holds the Buy Box, DSP traffic generates clicks and detail-page views, but purchases flow to that seller. The advertiser's own sales figures do not improve, and new-to-brand (NTB) purchase metrics reflect no conversion even though the buyer completed a transaction on the same PDP.

An out-of-stock ASIN is an extreme case: it has no Buy Box at all and displays no purchase option. A DSP ad that promotes an out-of-stock ASIN cannot produce any purchase regardless of click volume or creative quality.

A few signs point to a Buy Box issue:
  • The ASIN's PDP shows a seller name other than the advertiser's brand under the 'Sold by' or 'Ships from' field in the Buy Box
  • The ASIN shows an 'Currently unavailable' or similar message with no Buy Box present
  • The DSP campaign reports high click volume and normal detail-page-view rates but unusually low or zero purchase conversions
  • New-to-brand purchase volume is near zero despite prospecting line items running against new-audience segments
You may want to check Buy Box status on each promoted ASIN before launch and at regular intervals during a campaign flight. Buy Box eligibility can change when a competitor reprices or when inventory runs low.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For endemic advertisers, Buy Box status is the single most direct gateway between a DSP click and a recorded purchase. You may want to confirm that the advertiser holds the Buy Box on each promoted ASIN before the campaign goes live and set up a monitoring process for the duration of the flight. Promotions, price changes by third-party sellers, or an inventory shortage can shift Buy Box eligibility mid-campaign without any change in the DSP setup.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Non-endemic advertisers do not promote ASINs on Amazon.com and do not have a Buy Box. This check applies to endemic advertisers only.

investigate.intro: Confirm that the advertiser holds the Buy Box on each ASIN the campaign promotes, and that the ASIN is in stock with an active purchase option.

amazon_product_listing.label: Amazon product listing (manual review)
steps: Open the product detail page on Amazon.com for each ASIN the campaign promotes.
Locate the 'Buy Now' and 'Add to Cart' section on the right side of the page. Check whether the 'Sold by' or 'Ships from' field names the advertiser's brand or a third-party seller.
If a third-party seller name appears, the advertiser does not currently hold the Buy Box. Note the seller name.
If the page shows 'Currently unavailable' or a similar message with no purchase option, the ASIN is out of stock or suppressed. Note the ASIN and status.
Repeat for each ASIN promoted in the campaign.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: In the Amazon DSP console, pull purchase rate alongside click-through rate and detail-page-view rate for line items promoting the affected ASINs.

A line item with normal or healthy click-through and detail-page-view rates alongside near-zero purchases points toward a Buy Box or out-of-stock issue on the destination PDP rather than a creative or targeting problem.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per promoted ASIN, whether the advertiser holds the Buy Box and whether the ASIN is in stock with an active purchase option. An ASIN where the advertiser holds the Buy Box and the listing shows a normal in-stock status passes. An ASIN where a third-party seller holds the Buy Box, or where the listing is out of stock or shows no purchase option, is a critical fail recorded with the ASIN and the observed status.
evaluate.fallback_message: Review each promoted ASIN above and record the Buy Box holder and stock status to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} does not hold the Buy Box on one or more ASINs receiving traffic from the Amazon DSP campaign. When a third-party seller or Amazon holds the Buy Box on a promoted ASIN, clicks from the campaign arrive at the product detail page but purchases flow to that seller rather than to {{site_name}}. The campaign bears the full ad cost with no corresponding revenue benefit. For any out-of-stock ASINs in the campaign, there is no purchase path available at all. {{voice_recommend}} confirming Buy Box status on every promoted ASIN before launch and establishing a process to monitor it during the campaign flight. A competitor repricing or an inventory shortage can shift eligibility mid-campaign with no change in the DSP setup.

audit_checks.title: Promoted ASINs have a review count or star rating below the threshold that supports conversion
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Buyers evaluating a product on Amazon weigh star rating and review count as part of the purchase decision. An ASIN with fewer than approximately 15 reviews or a star rating below 3.5 gives buyers less social proof than competing listings, and DSP traffic that arrives at a low-rated PDP is less likely to convert.

educate.base:
Amazon product detail pages display a star rating and a review count prominently near the product title. Both figures are visible in Amazon DSP's Responsive eCommerce Creative (REC) ad units, which pull live data from the ASIN's listing. A buyer who clicks on a DSP ad and lands on the PDP sees the same rating and review count that appeared in the creative.

Review count and star rating influence whether a buyer continues toward a purchase or returns to the search results to evaluate a competing product. An ASIN with a very low review count, such as fewer than 15, gives buyers little social proof regardless of price or content quality. An ASIN with a star rating below 3.5 signals a product with unresolved quality issues. DSP spend that drives traffic to either situation produces clicks that the PDP cannot convert at a normal rate.

This check does not suggest abandoning a product launch because it has no reviews. A new ASIN may have legitimate reasons to run DSP campaigns before reviews accumulate, and Amazon's Vine program and other review-generation paths are available for eligible sellers. The check is about confirming that the practitioner is aware of the review state of each promoted ASIN and that campaign performance expectations are calibrated accordingly.

A few signs point to a review gap affecting conversion:
  • The ASIN shows fewer than 15 reviews in total
  • The ASIN's star rating is below 3.5, indicating buyer dissatisfaction at scale
  • The ASIN has a high detail-page-view rate relative to purchase rate, and review count or rating is the most visible differentiator from competing listings on the same search results page
You may want to review the star rating and review count for each promoted ASIN alongside its detail-page-view-to-purchase conversion rate before attributing underperformance to the campaign itself.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For endemic advertisers, the review state of a promoted ASIN is a signal about the PDP's ability to convert the traffic the campaign sends. An ASIN with a strong review count and a rating above 4.0 is in a better position to convert DSP traffic than a comparable ASIN with few reviews or a sub-3.5 rating. You may want to prioritize DSP budget toward ASINs with a stronger review profile when the product catalog includes both established and emerging listings.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Non-endemic advertisers do not promote Amazon ASINs and do not have star ratings or review counts on their destination pages. This check applies to endemic advertisers only.

learn_more.source_title: eCommerce display creatives: guidelines and ad specs

investigate.intro: Check the star rating and review count for each ASIN the campaign promotes, then compare the detail-page-view-to-purchase rate for ASINs with strong versus weak review profiles.

amazon_product_listing.label: Amazon product listing (manual review)
steps: Open the product detail page on Amazon.com for each ASIN the campaign promotes.
Note the star rating displayed under the product title and the total number of customer reviews. Record both for each ASIN.
Flag any ASIN with fewer than 15 reviews or a star rating below 3.5.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: In the Amazon DSP console, pull detail-page-view rate and purchase rate at the line item level, broken out by ASIN where reporting granularity allows.

Compare conversion rates across ASINs with different review profiles. An ASIN with a weak review count or low rating that also shows a low purchase rate relative to detail-page views is a candidate for pausing until the review situation improves or for replacing with a better-reviewed ASIN in the creative.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per promoted ASIN, the current star rating and review count. An ASIN with 15 or more reviews and a star rating at or above 3.5 passes. An ASIN with fewer than 15 reviews or a star rating below 3.5 is a flagged item recorded with the ASIN, the review count, and the rating observed. The practitioner notes whether the campaign performance expectations have been adjusted to reflect the ASIN's review state.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the star rating and review count for each promoted ASIN above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more ASINs receiving traffic from {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP campaign have a review count or star rating below the threshold that supports conversion. Star rating and review count are visible to buyers on the product detail page and appear in Amazon's Responsive eCommerce Creative ad units, so a low-rated or under-reviewed ASIN signals limited social proof to buyers who arrive from the campaign. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the star rating and review count for each promoted ASIN and calibrating campaign performance expectations accordingly. For ASINs with fewer than 15 reviews, you may want to consider whether running DSP spend against those listings is appropriate at this stage, or whether budget would be better allocated to ASINs with a stronger review profile while the lower-reviewed ASINs build social proof through Amazon Vine or other eligible review programs.
